problem_statement: Vibe coding—generating code via conversational prompts to LLMs—accelerates development but introduces frequent unstable code changes, reduced readability, verification gaps, and a lack of accountability/traceability; the paper addresses how to add lightweight human oversight to keep AI-assisted programming reliable and auditable.
insufficiency_evidence: Agentic systems 'require well-formed test suites to verify correctness' and 'risk human detachment'; self-refinement 'AI self-criticism can be shallow, repeating the same mistakes'; 'verification is fragile when test suites are weak' (UTBoost/SWE-Gym); Copilot studies 'reveal trade-offs: increased code churn, lower readability, and a need for vigilant oversight'
solution_mechanisms: prompt naming/tagging conventions (init-, refactor-, verify-, polish-, doc-)
verification before trust (manual, linting, or lightweight testing)
prompt logs and tagged commits as metadata layer
human as ultimate arbiter of quality
